name: Natural Twist Turn
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade, Back Corte, LF Rock
notes: Note: Amount of Turn: A complete turn to the right. Other variations: 7/8 to R could be made to end facing LOD. The following Promenade would move DC. 3/4 to R could be made at a corner or at the side of the room to end facing DC. The following Promenade would move to center. When following with Back Corté or LF Rock Man will turn a further 1/4 to face partner. Note: Amount of Turn: a complete turn to R. 2 styles (below) to finish this figure, either is ok, but they all finish in the same alignment. (Tendency is LF is not swiveled enough.) 5-step style finish: (traditional way), Lady pivot on LF on step 6. Pivoting on LF, it needs to pivot enough to have LF finish in the correct position (tendency is underturn). The weight is in transition. When swivel finishes, full weight is transferred onto LF. 4-step style finish, Lady pivot on RF on step 5. Pivoting on RF on step 5, then place LF parallel to RF in the Promenade Position.

comments: Keep the lane, Man in lane1 and lady in lane2.
Do not cross lady too early.

steps.action: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP
Start to twist R allowing feet to uncross
Feet almost closed weight on RF in PP
LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP

comments: Not too aggressive on this step.
There is slight rise on R hip /R side when Man cuts off in front.

steps.action: LF fwd preparing to step outside partner L side leading
comments: Keep left side up and forward, do not lift shoulder.
Keep tangent of circle, curving steps not as big.
Left shoulder is "walking around", dancing outside the circle. Don't drop left side/shoulder. Head follows the arm.

steps.action: LF to side small step in PP
comments: Stay behind Man. Don't drop left side/shoulder.
Head turning to PP: Don't turn head to R too early, don't turn head on its own too late either. (Sharpness depends on the lead, could be smooth turn or sharp head turn.)
